articles.title: 'We share same ambition' - Alonso named Chelsea's third boss in year
articles.summary: Former Liverpool midfielder Xabi Alonso is appointed as Chelsea manager - a day after their FA Cup final defeat by Manchester City.

articles.title: Chelsea announce appointment of Alonso as manager
articles.summary: Chelsea have announced the appointment of Xabi Alonso as manager.

articles.title: From non-league to FA Cup hero - Semenyo caps rise with 'incredible' goal
articles.summary: The FA Cup tends to produce fairytale stories and Antoine Semenyo's sensational winning goal in this season's final provided the latest in chapter in the storied history of the world's oldest cup comp...

articles.title: 'Wow!' - Semenyo scores 'special' goal to win FA Cup for Man City
articles.summary: Antoine Semenyo scores a "special" goal as Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 win the FA Cup final.

articles.title: Celtic lift Premiership trophy after incredible ending
articles.summary: Watch Celtic lift the Scottish Premiership trophy for the fifth season in a row, denying Hearts on an astonishing final day.

articles.title: Man City beat Chelsea after brilliant Semenyo goal
articles.summary: Antoine Semenyo's brilliantly-improvised backheel is enough to give Manchester City a 1-0 win over Chelsea in the FA Cup final, as Pep Guardiola's side complete a domestic cup double at Wembley.

articles.title: Kane nets hat-trick as Bayern thrash Cologne
articles.summary: England captain Harry Kane scores his fourth hat-trick of the season as Bundesliga champions Bayern Munich thrashed Cologne in their last league game of the campaign.

articles.title: Manchester City lift WSL trophy after win at West Ham
articles.summary: Champions Manchester City ended their Women's Super League campaign on a high, beating West Ham with Khadija Shaw scoring twice, before lifting the trophy.

articles.title: Arsenal clinch second spot in WSL with win at Liverpool
articles.summary: Russo scores twice as Arsenal beat Liverpool to finish second in the WSL clinching an automatic spot in the Women's Champions League.

articles.title: 'They always deliver' - Man City savour 'magical' title
articles.summary: For the first time in seven seasons, the ribbons on the Women's Super League trophy are a different shade of blue after Manchester City's "magical" triumph.

articles.title: 'Menacing and threatening atmosphere' forced Hearts exit
articles.summary: Hearts say "the menacing and threatening atmosphere inside the stadium" forced them to leave Celtic Park immediately after the final whistle of their Scottish Premiership decider.

articles.title: 'Celtic gave me reasons to live' - but O'Neill unsure about staying on
articles.summary: Martin O'Neill says Celtic have given him "reasons to live" after their stunning Scottish Premiership triumph, but the 74-year-old insists he does not know whether he will stay on beyond this season.

articles.title: Mourinho completes unbeaten season with Benfica
articles.summary: Jose Mourinho completes an unbeaten league season with Benfica but has to settle for third place in the Portuguese top-flight.

articles.title: Liverpool must return to heavy metal football - Salah
articles.summary: Mohamed Salah says Liverpool must return to being a "heavy metal attacking team that opponents fear" after trophyless season.

articles.title: Truth stranger than fiction as O'Neill leads Celtic from hostility to happiness
articles.summary: Martin O'Neill said Celtic would have to win seven out of seven in the run-in to win the Scottish Premiership - here is how they did it.

highlights.title: Premier League highlights this week - Week Highlights

articles.title: Tipped for relegation to title winners - AGF's 'King Arthur' moment
articles.summary: Aarhus GF were tipped for relegation from the Danish Superliga this season. Instead, they will lift the championship trophy for the first time in 40 years this Sunday.

articles.title: McIlroy, Rahm chasing as Smalley grabs shock lead in bunched major
articles.summary: Alex Smalley tops a congested leaderboard heading into the final day of the PGA Championship, with Rory McIlroy and Jon Rahm among the major stars in the chasing pack.

articles.title: Woad races into LPGA Tour lead as Hull's title hopes disappear
articles.summary: England's Lottie Woad will take a three-shot lead into the final round of the LPGA Tour's Kroger Queen City Championship in Ohio.

articles.title: 'I've still got a chance!' - McIlroy 'climbs out of hole' at PGA Champs
articles.summary: Rory McIlroy insists he still has a chance to challenge for back-to-back major titles after ‘climbing out of a hole’ to charge into PGA Championship contention.

articles.title: Ronaldo's trophy wait continues as Al-Nassr upset in final
articles.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo's wait for a first major trophy with Al-Nassr continues after they are upset 1-0 at home by Gamba Osaka in the AFC Champions League Two final.

articles.title: Chopping trees and country music - how Paterson will prepare for Wembley
articles.summary: Martin Paterson will be chopping trees and listing to country music as he begins preparations for Notts County's League Two play-off final at Wembley.

articles.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
articles.summary: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.
